description: Master structural map of the ethical authority architecture for Beast System 3.0. Defines ethical decision trees, harm‑reduction logic, dignity invariants, and cross‑layer authority constraints.

dignity_invariants.purpose: Defines the non‑negotiable dignity standards that all governance decisions must uphold.
invariants: no decision may reduce human dignity
no decision may create punitive outcomes when alternatives exist
every decision must preserve safety, autonomy, and wellbeing

harm_reduction_logic.purpose: Ensures all decisions follow trauma‑preventive pathways.
rules: punitive outcomes require exhaustion of all non‑punitive options
trauma indicators override standard decision pathways
harm‑reduction must be prioritized over procedural convenience

ethical_decision_tree.purpose: Defines the deterministic logic used to evaluate ethical pathways.

authority_constraints.purpose: Ensures authority is exercised ethically and predictably.
rules: no authority may exceed its ring classification
no authority may bypass dignity invariants
no authority may create harm without federal‑grade justification
all authority must trace back to Ring 0

wellbeing_alignment.purpose: Ensures all ethical decisions align with wellbeing standards.

rules: all ethical decisions must be logged
no ethical log may be deleted or altered

federal_context.author: Pat Tarwater Jr.
federal_context.notes: Ethical Authority Layer is part of the federal record and must remain immutable.
